label: Semantic memory

node.label: Semantic memory
node.definition: Facts, concepts, and relations are organized independently of a specific episode.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Semantic memory is modeled as depending on Embeddings & distributed representations, Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas and feeding downstream nodes such as Memory lifecycle, forgetting & consolidation.
examples: atlas downstream link to Memory lifecycle, forgetting & consolidation
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Embeddings & distributed representations, Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas.

dependencies.rationale: Semantic memory stores, selects, or obtains information through Embeddings & distributed representations.
Semantic memory stores, selects, or obtains information through Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas.
Semantic memory stores, selects, or obtains information through Parametric memory.
